title: Continuous observation audit and governance cadence family defined
objective: Define the ten proof-producing LifeOS governance operations that let LIFEOS-012 run as a living system: three time cadences (Daily Pulse, Weekly Sync, Quarterly Refactor) and seven domain audits (health, drift, security, provenance, capability, cost, authority). Each operation observes named inputs, emits only a bounded decision, handles stale data explicitly, escalates to David through the Governance Agent, writes a proof-ledger record, defaults to non-action with a defined rollback, and performs no hidden mutation. This family only DEFINES the cadences as planning artifacts; it runs no cadence and mutates no production state.

grounding_sources.context_matrix: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv

execution_constraints.parent: LIFEOS-012 (LifeOS continuously improves under governed feedback)

gate_contract.statement: Every cadence has observed inputs, bounded decisions, stale-data handling, owner escalation, proof, rollback or non-action, and no hidden mutation.

work_items.decision: Define the daily heartbeat as a read-only observation of the transient tier and swarm-integrity graph that publishes a pulse status but takes no corrective action.
observed_inputs: redb transient scratchpad state (agent thoughts, terminal traces, state flags) surfaced via UDS or shared redb state files (Blueprint: Local AI Scratchpad; Context Matrix 'The Scratchpad (redb)')
state/heartbeat_lane_a.json and connector_state/heartbeat_lane_b.json (existing OPS-001 heartbeat surface reconciled as evidence)
RuVector MinCut live cut-values on the agent-communication graph (Blueprint sec.6 MinCut, O(n^{o(1)}))
work_items.stale_data_handling: If redb is acting as an application-level WAL because PostgreSQL is offline or latent, the pulse is reported degraded-with-buffered-writes; the buffered redb state is NOT treated as canonical durable truth (STORE-001 single-durable-writer) and is only reconciled after the next envctl forward flush.
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David on any MinCut sever event or on pulse-degraded persisting beyond one daily window.
work_items.proof: Appends a heartbeat proof record (lineage of schema lifeos-planning-spine.ops-heartbeat.v0) to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Default is non-action (observation only). Rollback per LIVING-001 plan: restore the archived operations plan; no production state changes.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: Reads redb/UDS/heartbeat surfaces only; writes only under generated/** and proof_records/**; performs no production mutation, no autonomous remediation, and no external action (blocked_paths honored).
grounded_in: Blueprint: redb scratchpad + UDS status; MinCut immune system

work_items.decision: Define the weekly reconciliation of the forward-only redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL flush and of capability/priority parity, observing sync completeness without forcing any cutover.
observed_inputs: envctl bridge flush status: redb -> embed(all-MiniLM-L6-v2) -> PostgreSQL forward-only sync of settled/significant states (Blueprint sec.5; Context Matrix 'envctl + nu_plugin')
BlobStore SHA-256 parity receipts from blobstore_parity.rs and the PgStore contract (ADOPT-001; STORE-001 migration_path)
capability_state/p0_priority_report.json (priority-lock and active control surface reconciled as evidence)
work_items.stale_data_handling: A durable class still living redb-durable (transitional per STORE-001) without a green live parity run is flagged cutover-pending; the audit never auto-cuts over and never dual-writes, honoring the STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant.
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David / LifeOS Data Agent when parity drift or unflushed significant state is detected.
work_items.proof: Writes a weekly-sync proof-ledger record referencing the parity receipt and flush digest to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Non-action (observation of forward-only sync). Rollback: restore the archived operations plan; no production state changes.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: No dual-write, no forced cutover, no baseline promotion; reads flush status and parity receipts only. Writes only under generated/** and proof_records/**.
grounded_in: Blueprint: envctl forward flush of significant states
STORE-001 forward-only sync + verified cutover invariants

work_items.decision: Define the quarterly architectural review as an ATAS/ESN forecast over COW-isolated staging branches that plans refactors but commits nothing to the main trunk during the audit.
observed_inputs: ATAS future-timeline forecasts: state diffs, resource burn, complexity spikes, failure nodes (Blueprint sec.4; Context Matrix 'The Architecture of Simulated Future Timelines')
Echo-State Network stability scores and Temporal Strange Attractor boundary state (Blueprint sec.4 ESN/Reservoir Computing)
COW staging-branch verification state cloned from current PostgreSQL rows (Blueprint sec.5 COW branching)
work_items.stale_data_handling: ESN initial conditions are re-seeded from current PostgreSQL state before any forecast; a forecast is never run on stale codebase state, because sensitivity to initial conditions would invalidate the attractor shape (Blueprint sec.3).
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David for any refactor that would be an irreversible or architectural change (LIFEOS-012: explicit human authority for irreversible change).
work_items.proof: Writes a quarterly-refactor proof-ledger record with the forecast digest and the discarded/retained branch ids to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Non-action in planning; every simulated COW branch is discarded (COW rollback) and none is merged during the audit. Rollback: restore the archived operations plan.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: Simulations run in COW-isolated database sandboxes that are never merged to the main trunk during the audit; no production mutation, no silent baseline promotion. Writes only under generated/** and proof_records/**.
grounded_in: Blueprint: ATAS ensemble ESN simulation + COW branching + RuvLTRA
LIFEOS-012 explicit human authority for irreversible change

work_items.decision: Define the storage/runtime health audit over db_health and the verification runtime, recommending idempotent recovery but never running it autonomously.
observed_inputs: db_health command output: DbHealth { status, db_path, applied_migrations, last_migration_version, schema_version } where status == degraded when applied_migrations < embedded_migration_count (LifeOS storage foundation)
state/verification_runtime_report.json (bun scripts/verify-planning-spine.mjs pass) reconciled as evidence
redb application-level WAL depth as PostgreSQL-latency shield (Blueprint redb WAL; Context Matrix 'Edge Orchestration with High-Frequency Buffering')
work_items.stale_data_handling: If db_health is unreachable, redb holds writes as WAL and the audit reports degraded; the last-known-good health snapshot is never reported as current.
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David when degraded persists or a PostgreSQL PITR gap is detected.
work_items.proof: Writes a health proof-ledger record capturing db_health fields and verification result to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Emits a bounded RECOMMENDATION to run db_migrate (idempotent) but does not run it; non-action is the default. Rollback: restore the archived operations plan.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: Reads db_health and verification reports only; never invokes db_migrate or any recovery command autonomously (autonomous remediation is a blocked_path).

work_items.decision: Define the drift audit over trajectory, dependency-graph, and design-token drift against the last verified baseline, never promoting a new baseline silently.
observed_inputs: ATAS boundary-constraint / spiral-out-of-bounds state and Temporal Strange Attractor shape (Blueprint sec.3-4)
RuVector MinCut weakest-link cut-values on the dependency graph and Causal Graph Verification edge coverage (Blueprint sec.6)
state/mvp_bundle_report.json cross-object integrity and scope boundaries reconciled as evidence
work_items.stale_data_handling: Drift is computed against the last green proof-ledger baseline; if that baseline is stale it is re-anchored to the last verified snapshot rather than silently promoted (LIFEOS-012 blocked_path: silent baseline promotion).
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David on any spiral-out-of-bounds trajectory or any attempted silent baseline promotion.
work_items.proof: Writes a drift proof-ledger record with attractor-boundary status and MinCut cut summary to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Non-action; deterministic MinCut isolation of a hallucinating agent cluster is a swarm-internal severance, not a production mutation. Rollback: restore the archived operations plan.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: Observation only; promotes no baseline and writes no production state. Writes only under generated/** and proof_records/**.
grounded_in: Blueprint: ATAS boundary constraints; MinCut; Causal Graph Verification

work_items.decision: Define the security audit over Cryptographic Witness Chain coverage and secret-output guards, treating any unwitnessed durable write as untrusted.
observed_inputs: Cryptographic Witness Chain (SHAKE256) coverage binding every durable code edit/fact to a source vector (Blueprint sec.6; Context Matrix 'The Mathematical Guardrail')
ADOPT-001 secret-output guards and bounded read-only MCP boundary (generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv)
STORE-001 secret placement invariant: secrets only in OS keyring; redb never holds secrets; PostgreSQL stores references not raw secrets
work_items.stale_data_handling: A claim with no historical witness root is treated as untrusted (never as passing); a stale witness root is re-verified against its source vector before the entry is accepted (Causal Graph Verification).
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David on any secret exposure or any unwitnessed durable write.
work_items.proof: Writes a security proof-ledger record recording witness-chain coverage and guard status to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Non-action; database rejection of unwitnessed/hallucinated entries is the built-in Causal Graph guard, not an audit mutation. Rollback: restore the archived operations plan.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: Never reads or writes secret values; secrets/** is a blocked_path. Observation and proof only.
grounded_in: Blueprint: Cryptographic Witness Chains SHAKE256 + Causal Graph Verification
ADOPT-001 secret-output guards / bounded read-only MCP

work_items.decision: Define the provenance audit over content-addressed SHA-256 identity and source commit/PR lineage, flagging any orphaned durable blob.
observed_inputs: BlobStore SHA-256 content identity parity across redb and PgStore backends (ADOPT-001; STORE-001 migration_path)
generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv source commit + PR lineage per capability (ADOPT-001, 11 rows)
Causal Graph historical-root edges proving each durable fact originates from a verified source vector (Blueprint sec.6)
work_items.stale_data_handling: Any durable blob lacking a matching SHA-256 identity and a source-commit/PR lineage is flagged orphaned and never assumed valid; lineage is re-checked, not inferred.
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David / LifeOS Data Agent on orphaned durable data or a broken commit/PR lineage.
work_items.proof: Writes a provenance proof-ledger record with the checked SHA-256 identities and lineage refs to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Non-action; observation over lineage maps only. Rollback: restore the archived operations plan.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: Read-only over BlobStore identities and the capability lineage map; writes only under generated/** and proof_records/**.
grounded_in: Blueprint: SHA-256 content identity; Causal Graph historical root

work_items.decision: Define the capability audit that confirms every adopted capability and every state class is owned exactly once with a green parity gate and no downgrade.
observed_inputs: generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv: each capability appears once (no second-implementation row) with a parity gate (ADOPT-001, 11 rows)
STORE-001 state_class_assignments: six durable/transient/cognition/projection/secret classes each with exactly one canonical_owner
state/mvp_bundle_report.json cross-object integrity and scope boundaries (v0 in-scope / rfc proposed / post_v0 deferred)
work_items.stale_data_handling: A capability whose parity receipt is stale is treated as unverified (not passing); no capability is reported adopted without a current green receipt.
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David on any capability loss (downgrade) or duplicate implementation row.
work_items.proof: Writes a capability proof-ledger record enumerating parity-gate status per capability/class to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Non-action; observation over the capability and state-class maps only. Rollback: restore the archived operations plan.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: Read-only over the capability map and STORE-001 assignments; enforces upgrades-not-downgrades by observation, never by mutation.
grounded_in: ADOPT-001 capability map (once each, parity gate)

work_items.decision: Define the cost audit over ATAS resource-burn forecasts and RuvLTRA routing tiers, recommending cheaper routing but never re-routing autonomously.
observed_inputs: ATAS Resource Burn forecast: tokens, API dollars, compute cycles, and refactor-lock risk (Blueprint sec.4; Context Matrix 'Simulated Future Timelines')
RuvLTRA proportional-intelligence routing tier distribution: local ruvllm/Haiku reflex vs cloud Opus/GPT-4 escalation (Blueprint RuvLTRA)
actual token/API/compute telemetry versus the forecast bound
work_items.stale_data_handling: If actual burn telemetry is stale, the audit uses the conservative (higher) forecast bound rather than assuming the prior window's budget still holds.
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David on projected budget exhaustion or refactor-lock risk.
work_items.proof: Writes a cost proof-ledger record with forecast-vs-actual burn and routing mix to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Emits a bounded RECOMMENDATION to re-route via RuvLTRA to a cheaper tier but does not re-route; non-action is the default. Rollback: restore the archived operations plan.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: Read-only over burn telemetry and routing state; never changes routing policy autonomously (autonomous remediation is a blocked_path).
grounded_in: Blueprint: ATAS resource-burn + refactor-lock; RuvLTRA proportional intelligence

work_items.decision: Define the authority audit that re-verifies the STORE-001 single-durable-writer model, verifier/executor separation, and David's exclusive authority over irreversible change.
observed_inputs: STORE-001 invariants: single durable writer (PostgreSQL owns each durable macro-state class once; redb is never a second durable writer), forward-only sync, verified cutover, no-sidecar, provenance preserved
state/authority_integrity_report.json: verifier agent distinct from executor, verifier carries verifier role and cap.verify.proof (reconciled as evidence)
LIFEOS-012 authority boundary: explicit human (David) authority for irreversible change; no self-authorized expansion
work_items.stale_data_handling: Any authority claim not backed by a current authority_integrity_report is treated as unverified; verifier/executor separation and single-writer ownership are re-checked each run, never assumed from a prior pass.
work_items.owner_escalation: LifeOS Governance Agent raises to David on any single-durable-writer violation, redb-second-durable-writer, unverified cutover, or self-authorized expansion attempt.
work_items.proof: Writes an authority proof-ledger record recording invariant status and verifier-separation result to proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl.
work_items.rollback_or_non_action: Non-action; any irreversible change requires explicit David authorization and is never self-authorized. Rollback: restore the archived operations plan.
work_items.no_hidden_mutation: Read-only over STORE-001 invariants and the authority-integrity report; promotes no baseline and authorizes no expansion of its own scope.
grounded_in: STORE-001 single-durable-writer + forward-only + verified-cutover + no-sidecar invariants
LIFEOS-012 explicit human authority for irreversible change

completion_gate: All ten operations (three time cadences plus seven domain audits) are defined; each carries the seven required clauses (observed inputs, bounded decisions, stale-data handling, owner escalation, proof, rollback-or-non-action, no hidden mutation); every input traces to the Architecture Blueprint, the Architecture Context Matrix, STORE-001, ADOPT-001, or an existing reconciled evidence surface; and LIVING-001.proof.json records zero production mutation and zero autonomous remediation.
